candidates.label: Homebrew updates
candidates.description: Frequent brief posts announcing progress on homemade alcohol.

citations.text: More home brewed hooch on the way!
The brew closet is double booked

candidates.scope_note: Limited to the observed batch; other batches may differ.
candidates.boundary_note: Posts describing commercial beer production are not captured by this pattern.

candidates.label: Political voting frustration
candidates.description: Recurrent expressions of annoyance with being told how to vote or political encouragement.

citations.text: I'm not sure why everybody on Facebook keeps telling me who to vote for, I won't remember. Just write it on a $100 bill and give it to me next time you see me, that's much more effective.
STOP TELLING ME TO VOTE! I DO WHAT I WANT!
Don't forget to go out and vote today! #vote2012

candidates.scope_note: Only the examples in this batch support this observation.
candidates.boundary_note: Posts that simply report political news are not covered.

candidates.label: Short declarative statements
candidates.description: Single‑clause, minimal context posts often ending with an exclamation or period.

citations.text: You beat me this time, but I will return!

candidates.scope_note: Limited to the examples in this batch.
candidates.boundary_note: Multi‑sentence status updates do not fall under this pattern.
